response:
The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. They need to determine the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be calculated using the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from 52 is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is $ \binom{36}{5} $ (since there are 36 non-heart cards). The probability of getting no hearts in 5 cards is $ \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $. Therefore, the probability of getting at least one heart is $ 1 - \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $. This expression is a closed form and represents the desired probability.

***  

The author has two strategies to choose from. Strategy A has an expected value of $ 0.4 \times 1000 - 0.6 \times 500 = 400 - 300 = 100 $. Strategy B has an expected value of $ 0.6 \times 800 - 0.4 \times 200 = 480 - 80 = 400 $. The expected value of Strategy B is higher, so the author should choose Strategy B. 

Bagged with wisdom, the author learns that calculated risks often yield better outcomes. Bagged with caution, the author remains mindful of the probabilities at hand.
